attempt.claim: Integer-forcing lemma (verified): for a cone certificate of 1/b, if the local congruences C_j + b*sum_{i~j} q_i^{-1} ≡ 0 mod q_j hold at every auxiliary vertex AND 0<T<2 (T=normalized mass), then T=1 automatically — the certificate is exact. So fresh-1/b reduces to: build a local-congruence graph with mass T<2. PRECISE OBSTRUCTION found: the Dirichlet/CRT route is blocked — in any finite cone, the last auxiliary prime must DIVIDE a fixed integer (not lie in an AP), so Dirichlet doesn't apply.
depends_on: OPEN crux: height-controlled local-congruence graph with T<2 avoiding the Dirichlet trap (last prime divides fixed integer). This is the precise remaining construction step.
attempt.detail: Integer-forcing: local congruences => Q|N => T integer; 0<T<2 => T=1. One-prime INDUCTION DEAD: for b=2p, p≡1 mod3, p>3, subset sums are {0,p,2,p+2} and p+2≡0 mod3 is composite => no one-auxiliary fresh certificate (infinitely many such p). So any induction needs >=2 auxiliary primes + an edge. STRUCTURAL: for omega(b)>=3 every 1/b certificate MUST be a cone (no target-target edges, since b/(pp')>=2). COMPUTATION: every squarefree 2<=b<=1000 has a cone certificate (k<=3 covers all non-prime b; 20 prime cases via K4-minus-edge). New verified explicit certs: 1/397 (7 terms), 1/457 (6), 1/773 (5), 1/787 (7). NOT a solve; arbitrary forbidden set F still open. NEXT: height-controlled local-congruence lemma — build the graph with T<2 WITHOUT the last-prime-divides-a-fixed-integer trap.
attempt.insight: independent Opus verification: all 4 new certs exact-rational + distinct semiprimes; integer-forcing lemma sound.

provenance.proposer: GPT-5.5 Pro (integer-forcing round), verified by Opus

attempt.reusable_for: cone-certificate construction; local congruence + integer-forcing; Egyptian-fraction gadget graphs
